scenario: Yarrow Bank composes appointment slots on Camunda 8.8 across 6000 tasks per day and stores a separate date("2012-12-24") and time("T23:59:00") on the process instance. A modeller needs the documented two-argument signature of date and time on the conversion functions page that returns a date and time built from a date component combined with a time component on the process variable.
question: What does the conversion functions page document as the two-argument signature of date and time that combines a date and a time?

options.text: date and time(year: number, month: number, day: number): date and time; the docs document a numeric-components signature that takes three numbers for year, month, and day and returns a date and time on the conversion functions page

date and time(date: date, time: time): date and time; the docs document a two-argument signature that combines the date component of the first argument with the second time argument, and show date and time(date("2012-12-24"), time("T23:59:00")) returning date and time("2012-12-24T23:59:00")

date and time(string: string, format: string): date and time; the docs document a two-argument signature that parses an input string using an explicit format pattern and returns a date and time on the conversion functions page

date and time(date: date, offset: string): date and time; the docs document a two-argument signature that builds a date and time value from a date input combined with a timezone offset string argument given on the call on the conversion functions page

a.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a numeric-components signature for date and time; numeric components are documented for date(year, month, day), not date and time.

b.text: Correct. The conversion functions page documents date and time(date: date, time: time): date and time as a two-argument signature that returns a value with the date component of the first argument combined with the second time. It shows date and time(date("2012-12-24"), time("T23:59:00")) returning date and time("2012-12-24T23:59:00").

c.text: Incorrect. The conversion functions page does not document a string-with-format signature on date and time; the from-string signature takes a single string argument without a format pattern argument.

d.text: Incorrect. The two-argument signature combining a date with a timezone string is a separate signature, date and time(date: date and time, timezone: string), rather than the date-plus-time combination signature.

explanation: date and time(date: date, time: time): date and time and date and time(date: date and time, time: time): date and time return a date and time value composed of the date component of the first argument combined with the second time argument. The page shows date and time(date("2012-12-24"), time("T23:59:00")) returning date and time("2012-12-24T23:59:00").
